commit_note: Commit SHA is reported in the final JSON after committing this evidence file.

requirement_results.summary: Renderer contract exposes typed readPending() backed by governance_pending and returning pendingCount.
Renderer contract exposes readAccess() and derives hasAdminWrite/isReadOnly from governance_status_read authorities.
Renderer contract exposes commitPending(); backend governance_commit creates a real target-ref commit with message chore: update governance config.
No renderer direct governance TOML write path introduced. Raw grep count is zero; BSD wc pads the count, so the exact grep '^0$' suffix emits no line on this host.
Vitest proves pendingCount and hasAdminWrite are available through the typed renderer service without production stubs.
Rust IPC test proves commit action reaches a real Tauri command and commits only governance config files; Vitest proves renderer calls governance_commit.

green_evidence.command: cargo test -p gitbutler-tauri --test mgmt_ipc_003_governance_commands --test mgmt_ipc_005_governance_pending --test tt_022_governance_commit
pnpm --dir apps/desktop exec vitest run --mode development src/lib/governance/governanceService.test.ts

notes: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile was required because this fresh sprint worktree had no node_modules.
pnpm -F @gitbutler/shared package and pnpm -F @gitbutler/ui package were required before desktop check because those workspace packages export dist files.

A broad pnpm -F @gitbutler/desktop test -- src/lib/governance/governanceService.test.ts run executed unrelated desktop tests and failed on pre-existing workspace package resolution before package dist builds; the direct focused Vitest command passed.
